provenance: Local Decipher fixture derived from public Zodiac 340 transcriptions for transform/homophonic capability testing.
notes: Zodiac 340 solved plaintext fixture using local Zenith ciphertext and known Zenith transform pipeline. Context layers deliberately do not expose the plaintext, key, or known transform pipeline.

minimal.label: Minimal record context
minimal.text: This is the Zodiac 340 ciphertext, a 340-symbol cipher message attributed to the Zodiac correspondence in 1969. The benchmark task provides the ciphertext transcription only; do not assume access to the plaintext or known transform pipeline.

standard.label: Standard cipher metadata
standard.text: The ciphertext is English-language Zodiac 340 material with 340 cipher tokens and 63 distinct glyph IDs. The cipher family is believed to combine homophonic substitution with a transposition or route-order component, so a useful solve attempt should consider both symbol-to-letter assignment and ciphertext reading order.

historical.label: Historical/background context
historical.text: The Zodiac ciphers are associated with letters attributed to the Zodiac Killer in 1969-1970. This background may inform likely plaintext genre, tone, and spelling, but this context layer intentionally does not reveal the accepted plaintext or the known transform sequence.

provenance: Local Decipher neutral-alias fixture for testing context sensitivity on a solved 340-token public cipher transcription. Model-visible context intentionally avoids the public case name, accepted plaintext, key, and transform sequence.
notes: Neutral alias for a solved 340-token transposition/homophonic fixture. The scoring plaintext is available to the harness only; the target solution remains blocked from agent tools.

minimal.label: Minimal neutral context
minimal.text: This is a 340-token historical ciphertext transcription with 63 distinct glyph IDs. The benchmark task provides the ciphertext only. Do not assume access to plaintext, key material, or any known reading-order transform.

standard.label: Technical neutral context
standard.text: The plaintext language is believed to be English. The symbol inventory and length make simple substitution, homophonic substitution, and possible reading-order or grid-transposition hypotheses worth testing. Treat all solver outputs as hypotheses until the text reads as coherent prose.

historical.label: Historical neutral context
historical.text: The ciphertext comes from a public late-20th-century correspondence corpus. Background details are intentionally withheld in this neutral alias so the run measures tool use and cryptanalytic reasoning rather than public-case recognition.
